description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, light-colored stone with a rough, uneven surface and a generally triangular profile. The stone displays a pale beige to off-white coloration, with subtle variations and small specks of darker mineral inclusions scattered across its surface. One side features a small, rectangular paper label with faded writing, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The edges are jagged and natural, lacking any signs of artificial shaping or smoothing. The stone's surface texture is coarse, with visible grain and minor pitting, indicating exposure to natural weathering processes. Overall, it appears to be a medium-sized, solid rock fragment suitable for use as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban environment.
description: A rough, pale beige stone with an irregular, jagged shape and a coarse, grainy surface, featuring a small paper label on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its triangular profile and the attached paper label.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the stone's rounded edge and the subtle mineral speckling on its surface, with no visible label.
description_view_2: The stone appears more squat and compact from this side, emphasizing its rough, uneven texture and natural contours.
description_view_3: This view displays a relatively flat, slanted face with visible grain and minor pitting, lacking any labels or markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier along a path
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and speckling from mineral inclusions, with no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and naturally weathered, with minor pitting and surface grain visible, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces, may be partially embedded in soil or surrounded by grass.
